steps.title: Identify the key clue about movement
steps.content: Esteban walked straight to his seat "without looking at the rest of us" — he moved with focused, undeviating purpose toward one destination. The word must mean directly or without detour.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean moving in a direct, undeviated path toward a destination. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — justly: "walked justly to his seat" — justly means fairly or righteously. This describes moral conduct, not physical direction. ✗
  • Choice B — directly: "walked directly to his seat" — directly means without stopping or turning aside, perfectly matching the focused, undeviating movement described. ✓
  • Choice C — merrily: "walked merrily to his seat" — merrily means in a cheerful way. The scene is somber; Esteban sinks into his seat "sadly and heavily." ✗
  • Choice D — truthfully: "walked truthfully to his seat" — truthfully means honestly. This is a manner of speaking, not a way of walking. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
